excluded_defs.name: findPattern
excluded_defs.reason: read to confirm duplicate-check semantics, but the fix doesn't touch tree.go

exploration_log.aha_moment: Lines 324–328 already ensure both `/api` and `/api/` are registered when pattern doesn't end with `/`. The bug is simply that trailing-slash patterns bypass that block entirely. Stripping one character at the start fixes everything.
exploration_log.hindsight: Could have spotted the fix faster by reading line 324 first instead of tracing through the whole function.

alternatives: Modify the registration logic at line 324 to also handle trailing-slash inputs
Add a redirect from pattern→pattern+'/' in the handler
key_decisions.decision: Strip the trailing slash at the very start of Mount, before the findPattern duplicate check
key_decisions.reasoning: Normalizing early is the simplest and most consistent approach; all subsequent logic already works correctly for non-trailing-slash patterns.

search_sequence.reasoning: Task description pointed directly to Mount in mux.go as the location of the bug.
search_sequence.result: Found the `if pattern == "" || pattern[len(pattern)-1] != '/'` guard at line 324 which registers the bare pattern and pattern+`/` only when the input pattern does NOT end with `/`. So passing `/api/` skips that registration entirely.

search_sequence.action: Searched mux_test.go for TestMuxBig, TestMuxMounts, and existing Mount call sites
search_sequence.reasoning: Need to understand which tests would need updating after the behavior change.
search_sequence.result: TestMuxBig at line 885 used `r.Route("/folders/", ...)` and explicitly expected `/folders` → 404; TestMuxMounts calls Mount with non-trailing-slash patterns.

solve_notes: Inserted a trailing-slash normalization block at the top of `Mount` (after the nil handler guard) that strips a trailing `/` from the pattern when it is longer than one character. This makes `Mount("/api/", h)` behave identically to `Mount("/api", h)` — the existing code already handles all registration (bare pattern, bare+`/`, and `pattern/*`) once the slash is gone. Also updated `TestMuxBig` which had explicitly asserted the old broken behavior (404 for `/folders` when mounted as `/folders/`).
task_text:
Fix Mount not stripping trailing slash from the mount pattern

When `r.Mount("/api/", subRouter)` is called with a trailing slash,
requests to `/api` (without trailing slash) return 404 instead of being
routed to the sub-router. The `Mount` method in `mux.go` stores the
pattern as-is in the radix tree via `addRoute` in `tree.go`, but the
tree's `findRoute` does not match a request path of `/api` against a
pattern of `/api/`. Fix `Mount` to normalize trailing slashes on the
mount pattern so that both `/api` and `/api/` resolve correctly.
